Guideline: Neutral. Icelandic does not distinguish formal/informal second-person pronouns the way other European languages do. Address users directly and naturally.
Professional and concise. Icelandic is a compact language — do not pad translations to match English length.
Icelandic has grammatical gender. Use natural Icelandic gender agreement; avoid awkward inclusive constructions.
Sentence case throughout. Only proper nouns and first word of sentence/label capitalized.
Straight double quotes ("example") as seen consistently in TM. Guillemets not used.
Standard Icelandic punctuation. Percent: 15% (no space). Decimal separator: comma (,). Thousands: period (.). Date: DD.MM.YYYY.
{variable} placeholders preserved unchanged. Coverage is 80% — for untranslated strings, retain the English source.
Icelandic uses compounds and strong declension: Launabil, Gagnasett, Starfsmat, Samanburðarhópur. Nouns decline in 4 cases — apply correct case endings.
Icelandic strongly resists loanwords. Prefer native compounds: Markaðslaun for benchmark, Gagnasett for dataset. PayAnalytics, KPI, API, CSV, p-value kept as-is.
Concise imperative or infinitive forms: Bæta við, Flytja út, Bera saman. Subject omitted.
Actions: Bæta við [noun], Eyða [noun], Flytja út [noun]. Confirmations: Ertu viss um að þú viljir...?

Pay gap=Launabil; Adjusted pay gap=Leiðrétt launabil; Unadjusted pay gap=Óleiðrétt launabil; Gender pay gap=Launabil (kynjabundið); Pay band=Launaspönn; Salary range=Launabili; Compensation=Laun; Salary=Laun; Raise=Hækkun; Benchmark=Markaðslaun; Pay equity=Jafnlaunagreining; Equal pay=Jafnlaunagreining; Job evaluation=Starfsmat; Workforce analytics=Jafnréttisvísar; Headcount=Fjöldi; Dataset=Gagnasett; Comparison group=Samanburðarhópur; Employee group=Flokkur; Maternity leave=Fæðingarorlof; Bonus=Bónusgreiðsla; Compa-ratio=Compa-ratio (kept)
+4% average length vs. English. Very compact — do not add words to fill space.
